description_long: This is a multi-story concrete hostel building with a utilitarian, weathered appearance, featuring a mix of flat and sloped corrugated metal roofs. The facade displays a variety of window sizes and arrangements, with some sections having large, rectangular windows framed in white and others with smaller, more irregularly placed windows. Several balconies and awnings protrude from the structure, some with metal railings and others with simple overhangs, adding to the building's layered look. The exterior walls are predominantly gray, showing signs of aging and exposure, with occasional patches of discoloration and grime. A metal tower-like structure is attached to one side, possibly serving as a fire escape or utility access. The ground level includes multiple entrances and shuttered sections, suggesting commercial or communal spaces at street level.
description: A weathered, multi-story concrete hostel with mixed flat and sloped roofs, varied window arrangements, and several balconies and awnings. The building has a utilitarian, urban appearance with visible signs of aging and a metal tower structure on one side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with large windows, multiple balconies, and a prominent sloped metal roof section.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the attached metal tower, flat roof sections, and a mix of window sizes and placements.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by plain concrete walls with minimal windows and the sloped roof visible at the top.
description_view_3: Alternate front view showing the main entrance area, large windows, and the overhanging roof structure.
attribute_entrance_design: The main entrance is located at the ground level beneath the overhanging roof, featuring wide doorways with metal shutters and a small awning above. The entrance area is set back slightly from the facade, with no visible steps or ramps, and is framed by concrete and metal elements.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in horizontal bands and vertical stacks, with large rectangular panes on the main facade and smaller, irregularly placed windows on the sides and rear. Some windows have white frames, while others are set directly into the concrete, and a few are partially covered by awnings or balcony structures.
attribute_balcony_presence: Balconies are present, primarily on the front and side facades. They vary in size and style, with some featuring metal railings and others simple concrete ledges. Awnings and overhangs are also present above some balconies.
attribute_architectural_style: The building exhibits a utilitarian, Brutalist architectural style with exposed concrete, minimal ornamentation, and functional design elements.
